reasoning: The question asks if there will be at least one cube-shaped UFO sighting reported in June 2025 to NUFORC. The prompt notes that December 2024 had 6 cube sightings, but qualifies this by saying 6 in a single month is "rare in the history of NUFORC." This indicates that the typical number of cube-shaped sightings per month is much lower, possibly averaging around 0.5 to 2 per month. Summer months (like June) generally see higher overall UFO reporting rates due to more people spending time outdoors. Moreover, in recent years (since 2019-2020), "cube" has become a more popular shape in UFO lore due to Navy pilot accounts of "cubes inside spheres," which likely raises the baseline rate of "cube" classifications compared to earlier decades. Treating this as a Poisson process, if the expected return rate (lambda) of cube sightings per month is between 0.8 and 1.5, the probability of seeing at least one sighting is roughly 55% to 78%. Considering both the historical rarity and the recent uptick in cube prominence (typified by the 6 sightings in Dec 2024), an estimate of 65% balances the possibility of a month with zero sightings against the steady background rate of reports.
